text:
Two frogs sat on a fence post. The first frog spoke with a deep baritone. "Ah, what a lovely day!"

The second frog chirped, quicker. "Yes yes, certainly. Wouldn't you agree?"

A frog buzzed by. "Eww, very."

"No doubt." The first frog sighed. "You know, I've heard that place before. There used to be a toad there. He'd say things like that all the time."

"Heaven forbid!" A third frog paused. "Did you hear him?"

"Oh, a poet!" said the first.

"The stars?"

"Nope."

"It isn't? How intriguing."

Just then, a shadow passed.

"Oh! Somebody coming."

Quick as a whisper. "Why?"

"To listen."

Silent for a moment.

"Well?" the first frog prompted.

"To hear."

The sound crescendoed into moans and squeaks. "Oh dear. There's one there."
